sourceMovie.title: Saint Seiya: Legend of Crimson Youth
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Saint Seiya: Legend of Crimson Youth (1988) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Shigeyasu Yamauchi's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and animation blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mortal Kombat Legends: Scorpion's Revenge
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Saint Seiya: Legend of Crimson Youth clicked for you, Mortal Kombat Legends: Scorpion's Revenge (2020) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and animation, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Saint Seiya: Legend of Crimson Youth?
faq.answer: Try Mortal Kombat Legends: Scorpion's Revenge, My Hero Academia: Heroes Rising, Baki Hanma VS Kengan Ashura—each shares DNA with Saint Seiya: Legend of Crimson Youth while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Saint Seiya: Legend of Crimson Youth?
faq.answer: Saint Seiya: Legend of Crimson Youth (1988) is best described as Action, Animation, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Saint Seiya: Legend of Crimson Youth part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Saint Seiya Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
